message:
fix(privacy): add file locking for concurrent config access

Problem:
- Privacy command modifies global config (~/.cass-memory/config.json)
- Multiple concurrent processes could race when updating crossAgent settings
- enable/disable/allow/deny all perform read-modify-write operations
- Without locking, last write wins and intermediate changes are lost

Solution:
- Wrap all config mutations in withLock(globalConfigPath, ...)
- Reload config inside lock to ensure atomic read-modify-write
- Status action (read-only) remains unlocked for performance
- Lock scope is per-action, not for entire command duration

Affected operations:
- privacy enable: Sets crossAgent.enabled=true, updates allowlist
- privacy disable: Sets crossAgent.enabled=false
- privacy allow <agent>: Adds agent to allowlist
- privacy deny <agent>: Removes agent from allowlist

The pattern follows existing locking conventions used elsewhere
in the codebase (playbook saves, processed log, etc.).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
